fields.name: sla_policy_id
fields.notes: Auto-assigned based on queue and priority

computed.formula: IF resolved: check if resolved_at <= resolution_due_at; ELIF NOW() > resolution_due_at: 'breached'; ELIF NOW() > resolution_due_at * 0.8: 'at_risk'; ELSE: 'within_sla'
created_at + (sla_policy.first_response_hours * INTERVAL '1 hour') adjusted for business_hours_only schedule
created_at + (sla_policy.resolution_hours * INTERVAL '1 hour') adjusted for business_hours_only schedule

fields.name: satisfaction_rating
fields.notes: 1-5 CSAT rating from requester after resolution

fields.name: version
fields.notes: Optimistic lock version — incremented on every UPDATE

db_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

modules.name: AgentInbox
modules.description: Agent-facing ticket inbox sorted by SLA urgency

modules.name: AllTickets
modules.description: Complete ticket queue across all agents

modules.name: RequesterPortal
modules.description: Self-service portal for requesters to submit and track their tickets

modules.name: Analytics
modules.description: Support team performance and volume analytics

modules.name: Settings
modules.description: Configure queues, SLA policies, canned responses, and automations

GET /api/tickets/:id.description: Get ticket with replies and properties

events.name: ticket:created
events.description: Notify queue agents of new ticket

events.name: ticket:reply_added
events.description: Real-time thread update in ticket detail

events.name: ticket:status_changed
events.description: Update list view badge in real-time

events.name: ticket:sla_at_risk
events.description: Alert agent when ticket SLA enters at-risk window

events.name: ticket:sla_breached
events.description: Alert agent and manager on SLA breach

events.name: ticket:satisfaction_submitted
events.description: Notify agent of CSAT rating received

flows.name: Submit Ticket
flows.trigger: Requester submits form or sends email
steps: Auto-assign to queue based on ticket_type routing rules
Auto-assign to agent based on queue.auto_assign_mode
Determine and attach SLA policy based on queue + priority
Send confirmation email to requester with ticket number
Notify assigned agent via websocket and email

flows.name: Agent Reply
steps: If first public reply → set ticket.first_responded_at, mark is_first_response = true
If reply_type = public → send email notification to requester
If status changed (submit_and_close, submit_and_pending) → update ticket status

flows.name: SLA Monitoring
steps: Scan open tickets: check first_response_due_at and resolution_due_at vs NOW()
If within 80% of window → set sla_status = at_risk, broadcast ticket:sla_at_risk
If past due → set sla_status = breached, broadcast ticket:sla_breached, notify manager
If ticket resolved/closed → compute final SLA compliance result

flows.name: CSAT Survey
flows.trigger: Ticket resolved → send email after 24 hours
steps: Cron job sends CSAT email with 1-5 star link to requester
Requester clicks rating link → pre-authenticated token

computed_fields.name: first_response_due_at
computed_fields.formula: created_at + (sla_policy.first_response_hours * INTERVAL '1 hour') accounting for business_hours_only

computed_fields.name: resolution_due_at
computed_fields.formula: created_at + (sla_policy.resolution_hours * INTERVAL '1 hour') accounting for business_hours_only

computed_fields.name: sla_status
computed_fields.formula: CASE WHEN resolved → within_sla/breached; WHEN NOW() > due_at → breached; WHEN NOW() > due_at * 0.8 → at_risk ELSE within_sla END

computed_fields.name: avg_resolution_hours
computed_fields.formula: AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM resolved_at - created_at) / 3600) WHERE resolved_at IS NOT NULL

computed_fields.name: csat_avg
computed_fields.formula: AVG(satisfaction_rating) WHERE satisfaction_rating IS NOT NULL

domain_rules: Ticket number auto-increments per org: TKT-0001, TKT-0002
Internal notes are never visible to requesters (filtered server-side on requester portal)
Merged tickets: secondary ticket redirects to primary; replies preserved on primary
SLA timer pauses when status = waiting_on_requester or waiting_on_third_party
CSAT survey sent 24 hours after resolution; only one survey per ticket
Round-robin assignment tracks last assigned agent per queue to distribute evenly
Requester can reopen a resolved ticket within 7 days; after that requires agent action
Email ingestion: inbound emails create tickets; replies to ticket email threaded via ticket_number in subject
Agents can only see tickets in their assigned queues unless they have admin role
generation_rules: SLA due times stored on ticket at creation; not dynamically recomputed on every query
Business hours SLA: use work-schedule table (Mon-Fri 9am-5pm default) to compute business-hour intervals
SLA monitoring cron runs every 5 min; batch-updates sla_status in single UPDATE WHERE
Internal notes have reply_type = internal_note; server excludes them from requester API responses
Email ingestion via inbound email parsing service; route by subject ticket number pattern
Ticket number in all outbound emails: '[TKT-0042]' in subject for reply threading
Round-robin: store last_assigned_agent_idx per queue in Redis for fast lookup
CSAT email link uses signed JWT token valid 7 days; no login required to submit rating

org_id_injection.middleware: req.body.org_id is stripped; injected from req.user.org_id

conflict_response.message: Record was modified by another user. Refresh and retry.

concurrency.high_contention_tables: see entities with high write concurrency

audit_trail.triggers: application-level (before write hook) — never DB trigger to avoid log bloat

standard_constraints.name: chk_{table}_deleted_order
standard_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

db_constraints_policy.enum_validation: DB ENUM types preferred over VARCHAR + CHECK; enum values defined in global enums registry

soft_delete_policy.filter: deleted_at IS NULL applied globally via query builder middleware
soft_delete_policy.cascade: Parent delete soft-deletes children via application cascade, not DB cascade
soft_delete_policy.hard_delete: Prohibited in production; available only via admin CLI with audit log
soft_delete_policy.restore: Set deleted_at = NULL; triggers audit log entry

naming_conventions.date_fields: Always use _at suffix for TIMESTAMPTZ, _date suffix for DATE
naming_conventions.id_fields: Primary key is always id; foreign keys are {entity}_id
